results.title: Assessment Results

results.reviewInterventions: Review the priority interventions below for specific actions

HALST_1.question: What type of housing do you live in?
HALST_1.explanation: Different housing types have different health considerations

HALST_2.question: What floor do you live on?
HALST_2.explanation: Higher floors may have different air quality and accessibility considerations

HALST_3.question: How old is your building?
HALST_3.explanation: Building age affects structural integrity and potential health hazards

HALST_4.question: Does your home have ventilation (windows that open or ventilation fans)?
HALST_4.explanation: Proper ventilation is essential for indoor air quality

HALST_5.question: Are there any cracks in walls or ceilings?
HALST_5.explanation: Cracks can indicate structural problems and allow moisture entry

HALST_6.question: Are there any signs of water damage or leaks?
HALST_6.explanation: Water damage can lead to mold growth and structural problems

HALST_7.question: Is there peeling or chipping paint?
HALST_7.explanation: Peeling paint in older buildings may contain lead

HALST_8.question: Do you have problems with pests (insects, rodents)?
HALST_8.explanation: Pests can carry diseases and trigger allergies

HALST_9.question: Is there adequate lighting in your home?
HALST_9.explanation: Poor lighting can cause accidents and eye strain

HALST_10.question: Do you feel safe in your neighborhood?
HALST_10.explanation: Safety concerns can affect mental health and well-being

HALST_11.question: Is your bedroom well-ventilated?
HALST_11.explanation: Bedroom ventilation affects sleep quality and air quality

HALST_12.question: Do you have trouble sleeping due to noise?
HALST_12.explanation: Noise pollution can disrupt sleep and affect health

HALST_13.question: Is there adequate natural light in your bedroom?
HALST_13.explanation: Natural light helps regulate circadian rhythms

HALST_14.question: Is your bedroom temperature comfortable?
HALST_14.explanation: Temperature control affects sleep quality and comfort

HALST_15.question: Is there dampness or condensation in your bedroom?
HALST_15.explanation: Dampness can lead to mold growth and respiratory problems

HALST_16.question: Do you have adequate cooking facilities?
HALST_16.explanation: Proper cooking facilities are essential for nutrition and food safety

HALST_17.question: Is your kitchen well-ventilated?
HALST_17.explanation: Kitchen ventilation removes cooking fumes and moisture

HALST_18.question: Do you have access to clean drinking water?
HALST_18.explanation: Clean water is essential for health and hydration

HALST_19.question: Is there adequate food storage space?
HALST_19.explanation: Proper food storage prevents spoilage and pest problems

HALST_20.question: Are there any problems with your stove or cooking equipment?
HALST_20.explanation: Faulty cooking equipment can pose safety and health risks

HALST_21.question: Do you have hot water available?
HALST_21.explanation: Hot water is necessary for hygiene and sanitation

HALST_22.question: Is your bathroom clean and well-maintained?
HALST_22.explanation: Bathroom hygiene is important for preventing infections

HALST_23.question: Does your toilet flush properly?
HALST_23.explanation: Proper sanitation facilities are essential for health

HALST_24.question: Is there adequate privacy in your bathroom?
HALST_24.explanation: Privacy is important for dignity and comfort

HALST_25.question: Is your bathroom well-ventilated?
HALST_25.explanation: Bathroom ventilation prevents moisture buildup and mold

HALST_26.question: Are there any leaks in your bathroom?
HALST_26.explanation: Bathroom leaks can cause water damage and mold growth

HALST_27.question: Is there visible mold in your bathroom?
HALST_27.explanation: Mold can cause respiratory problems and allergic reactions

HALST_28.question: Is there adequate space for relaxation in your living area?
HALST_28.explanation: Personal space is important for mental health and well-being

HALST_29.question: Is there too much clutter in your living areas?
HALST_29.explanation: Clutter can create safety hazards and affect mental health

HALST_30.question: Do you have adequate heating in winter?
HALST_30.explanation: Proper heating is essential for comfort and health

HALST_31.question: Do you have adequate cooling in summer?
HALST_31.explanation: Temperature control prevents heat-related health problems

HALST_32.question: Is the air quality in your home generally good?
HALST_32.explanation: Poor air quality can cause respiratory and other health problems

HALST_33.question: Are there strong odors in your home?
HALST_33.explanation: Strong odors may indicate air quality or hygiene problems

HALST_34.question: Is there excessive dust in your home?
HALST_34.explanation: Dust can trigger allergies and respiratory problems

HALST_35.question: Are there any unusual sounds (like buzzing or humming)?
HALST_35.explanation: Unusual sounds may indicate electrical or mechanical problems

HALST_36.question: Does anyone smoke tobacco inside your home?
HALST_36.explanation: Indoor smoking creates serious health hazards for all residents

HALST_37.question: Do you have smoke detectors that work?
HALST_37.explanation: Working smoke detectors are essential for fire safety

HALST_38.question: Is there a first aid kit available?
HALST_38.explanation: First aid supplies are important for emergency medical care

HALST_39.question: Are electrical outlets and wiring in good condition?
HALST_39.explanation: Faulty electrical systems pose fire and shock hazards

HALST_40.question: Overall, how would you rate the health and safety of your housing?
HALST_40.explanation: Your overall assessment helps identify priority concerns

HALST_41.question: How comfortable do you feel in your home environment overall?
HALST_41.explanation: Overall comfort reflects environmental health and well-being

HALST_42.question: How often do you clean your home?
HALST_42.explanation: Regular cleaning helps maintain a healthy living environment

HALST_43.question: Do you have adequate storage space in your home?
HALST_43.explanation: Adequate storage helps prevent clutter and maintains organization

HALST_44.question: How would you rate the overall condition of your home?
HALST_44.explanation: Overall home condition affects health, safety, and quality of life

HALST_45.question: Do you feel your home meets your basic needs?
HALST_45.explanation: Meeting basic housing needs is fundamental for health and well-being

HALST_46.question: How satisfied are you with your current housing situation?
HALST_46.explanation: Housing satisfaction affects mental health and overall well-being

HALST_47.question: Do you have any major concerns about your housing?
HALST_47.explanation: Identifying housing concerns helps prioritize health and safety improvements

HALST_48.question: Overall, how satisfied are you with your home's environmental health?
HALST_48.explanation: Overall satisfaction with environmental health may indicate multiple health risks

US001.question: Is water draining away from the house?
US001.explanation: Water should drain away from foundation to prevent moisture problems

US002.question: Is the ground sloped away from the foundation?
US002.explanation: Proper grading prevents water from pooling near the foundation

US003.question: Are there areas of standing water near the house?
US003.explanation: Standing water can lead to pest problems and foundation issues

US004.question: Are walkways and driveways in good condition (no major cracks, level)?
US004.explanation: Uneven walkways pose trip hazards and safety risks

US005.question: Are trees and large branches a safe distance from the house?
US005.explanation: Trees too close to the house can cause damage during storms

US006.question: Is the roof in good condition (no missing shingles, sagging)?
US006.explanation: Roof damage can lead to water infiltration and structural problems

US007.question: Are gutters and downspouts clean and functioning properly?
US007.explanation: Blocked gutters can cause water damage and foundation problems

US008.question: Is exterior siding/walls in good condition (no cracks, holes)?
US008.explanation: Damaged exterior walls allow moisture and pests to enter

US009.question: Are windows properly sealed and caulked?
US009.explanation: Poor window sealing leads to drafts and energy loss

US010.question: Is the chimney in good condition (if applicable)?
US010.explanation: Damaged chimneys pose fire and carbon monoxide risks

US011.question: Are there signs of moisture, water damage, or flooding?
US011.explanation: Moisture in basements leads to mold and structural damage

US012.question: Are there visible cracks in the foundation walls?
US012.explanation: Foundation cracks can indicate serious structural problems

US013.question: Is there adequate ventilation in basement/crawlspace?
US013.explanation: Poor ventilation leads to moisture buildup and mold growth

US014.question: Are there signs of pests, rodents, or termites?
US014.explanation: Pests can spread disease and cause structural damage

US015.question: Has the home been tested for radon?
US015.explanation: Radon is a leading cause of lung cancer in non-smokers

US016.question: Is the heating system working properly?
US016.explanation: Faulty heating systems pose safety risks and health concerns

US017.question: Is the cooling/AC system working properly?
US017.explanation: Inadequate cooling can lead to heat-related health problems

US018.question: Are HVAC filters changed regularly (every 1-3 months)?
US018.explanation: Dirty filters reduce air quality and system efficiency

US019.question: Are carbon monoxide detectors installed near heating equipment?
US019.explanation: CO detectors prevent deadly carbon monoxide poisoning

US020.question: Are exhaust vents clear and functioning properly?
US020.explanation: Blocked vents can cause dangerous gas buildup indoors

US021.question: Is there adequate insulation in the attic?
US021.explanation: Poor insulation increases energy costs and comfort issues

US022.question: Is the attic properly ventilated?
US022.explanation: Attic ventilation prevents moisture buildup and ice dams

US023.question: Are there signs of roof leaks or water damage in attic?
US023.explanation: Attic water damage indicates roof problems requiring repair

US024.question: Are there signs of pests or animals in the attic?
US024.explanation: Attic pests can damage insulation and spread disease

US025.question: Is water pressure adequate throughout the home?
US025.explanation: Poor water pressure may indicate plumbing problems

US026.question: Are there visible leaks under sinks or around toilets?
US026.explanation: Water leaks can cause damage and promote mold growth

US027.question: Is the hot water heater functioning properly?
US027.explanation: Faulty water heaters can be dangerous and inefficient

US028.question: Is water heater temperature set at 120°F or below?
US028.explanation: High water temperature poses scalding risk, especially for children

US029.question: Do all drains empty quickly without backing up?
US029.explanation: Slow drains may indicate blockages or plumbing issues

US030.question: Are interior walls free from major cracks, holes, or water damage?
US030.explanation: Interior wall damage can indicate structural or moisture problems

US031.question: Are ceilings in good condition (no stains, sagging)?
US031.explanation: Ceiling problems often indicate roof leaks or structural issues

US032.question: Is the home free from peeling/chipping paint (lead hazard if pre-1978)?
US032.explanation: Lead paint poses serious health risks, especially to children

US033.question: Do all windows open, close, and lock properly?
US033.explanation: Window function affects security, ventilation, and emergency egress

US034.question: Do all doors open, close, and lock properly?
US034.explanation: Door function affects security and emergency evacuation

US035.question: Is there visible mold on walls, ceilings, or around windows?
US035.explanation: Visible mold indicates moisture problems and health risks

US036.question: Is the stove/oven working safely and properly?
US036.explanation: Faulty cooking equipment poses fire and gas leak risks

US037.question: Is the refrigerator maintaining proper temperature?
US037.explanation: Improper refrigeration can lead to foodborne illness

US038.question: Are washer and dryer properly installed and vented?
US038.explanation: Improper appliance installation poses fire and moisture risks

US039.question: Are all appliance electrical cords in good condition?
US039.explanation: Damaged electrical cords pose fire and shock hazards

US040.question: Are GFCI outlets installed in bathrooms and kitchen?
US040.explanation: GFCI outlets prevent electrical shock in wet areas

US041.question: Are all electrical outlets working and properly covered?
US041.explanation: Faulty outlets pose electrical shock and fire risks

US042.question: Is the electrical panel accessible and properly labeled?
US042.explanation: Proper electrical panel access is important for safety and maintenance

US043.question: Are smoke detectors installed on every level and tested monthly?
US043.explanation: Working smoke detectors are critical for fire safety

US044.question: Does garage door open/close properly with safety features working?
US044.explanation: Garage door safety features prevent injury and property damage

US045.question: Is there proper fire separation between garage and living space?
US045.explanation: Fire separation prevents garage fires from spreading to living areas

ELDER_1.question: Are there papers, books, shoes, or other objects on floors?
ELDER_1.explanation: Objects on floors create tripping hazards for seniors

SDOH_1.question: Within the past 12 months, did you worry that your food would run out before you got money to buy more?
SDOH_1.explanation: Food security is a key social determinant of health
